summary: Baselib-focused decomp/matching PR touching 14 files under src/sysdolphin/baselib, with major churn in hsd_3B34.c, particle.c, hsd_3B5C.c, hsd_3AA7.c, texpdag.c, and sislib.c. The PR body reports clean formatting/check/build/diff validation and no regression losses; the bot report records 9 new matches and many unmatched-item improvements.

bot_report: hsd_3AA7 functions fn_803B1338, fn_803ADF90, fn_803AD16C, fn_803B0E9C, fn_803B0120 improved
bot_report: SimplifyByMerge improved 74.48% to 99.99%; SimplifyThis, HSD_TExpMakeDag, and order_dag also improved

evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: moves hdr_plus_icon assignment into guarded branches; changes -1 comparisons to 0xFFFF; changes loop shape and byte-combine expression order

what_changed: Touched 14 files under src/sysdolphin/baselib: axdriver.c, hsd_3A94.c, hsd_3AA7.c, hsd_3AA7.h, hsd_3B2E.c, hsd_3B34.c, hsd_3B5C.c, leak.c, particle.c, psdisp.c, sislib.c, sobjlib.c, synth.c, and texpdag.c.
PR body describes the scope as implementation-focused C decomp/match improvements with one local baselib header update and no global build/config changes.

Diff excerpt shows hsd_3AA7.c adding local CARD_QUEUE_CMD and CARD_WRITE_BLOCK macros, inline helper boundaries for repeated CARD/block operations, explicit PAD_STACK uses, and a typed field access change from x70[file_idx] to x70[file_idx].size.

Diff excerpt shows hsd_3A94.c local matching edits including delayed hdr_plus_icon assignment, 0xFFFF sentinel comparisons, do-while loop reshaping, and expression order changes for big-endian byte combination.
Diff excerpt shows axdriver.c adding const data symbols AXDriver_803B95F8 and AXDriver_803B9618.
Bot report records 9 new full matches and 62 improvements in unmatched items, including particle, hsd_3B2E, hsd_3B5C, texpdag, synth, hsd_3AA7, hsd_3B34, sislib, and psdisp units.

smart_moves.text: Kept the review slice local to sysdolphin baselib and avoided broad build/config churn, reducing cross-system risk for a large matching PR.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Scope: sysdolphin baselib implementation files
pr_body: No global build/config changes are included

smart_moves.text: Paired large source churn with explicit validation claims and bot-reported match improvements, including build, dol_diff, changes_all, and a structured regression sweep.
evidence_refs: pr_body: ninja build/GALE01/main.dol OK; ninja dol_diff; ninja changes_all; regressions=0
bot_report: matched code +3508 bytes; 9 new matches

smart_moves.text: Introduced small local inline helpers in hsd_3AA7.c to isolate repeated block-count, payload/header pointer, CARDWrite, and CARDRead shapes instead of leaving all arithmetic and retry loops open-coded.
Used a real typed field where available in hsd_3AA7.c, changing x70[file_idx] to x70[file_idx].size.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For baselib matching work, small helper extraction can be useful when repeated offset arithmetic or retry-loop bodies appear in multiple local contexts, but the evidence here only supports this as a PR-local tactic, not a global rule.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: inline helpers added around block-count math, payload/header pointer math, CARDWrite, and CARDRead
decomp_standard: header-inlines says restore local inline helper boundaries when sibling functions share body shape and objdiff supports the helper call or inline

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a struct field type is known, prefer a typed field member over raw or aggregate slot access; hsd_3AA7.c demonstrates this with x70[file_idx].size.

Loop form and index base can be match-sensitive in CARD/baselib code: the diff changes for-loops to do-while or bounded for-pass forms, adjusts 0-based to 1-based iteration, and changes early-break behavior in hsd_3A94.c and hsd_3AA7.c.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: hsd_3A94.c for loop changed to do-while around file_info.fileNo
diff_excerpt: hsd_3AA7.c while(1) changed to for (pass = 0; pass <= state->x460; pass++)
diff_excerpt: hsd_3AA7.c for (i = 0; i < total_blocks; i++) changed to for (i = 1; i <= total_blocks; i++)

decomp_lessons.lesson: Literal spelling can be part of the source-shape hypothesis: hsd_3A94.c changed sentinel comparisons from -1 to 0xFFFF where command fields are compared.

naming_conventions.text: Local helper and macro names in hsd_3AA7.c remain address-based or operation-scoped rather than speculative semantic renames: fn_803AC634_remaining, fn_803AC7DC_block_count, fn_803ACFC0_payload_dst, fn_803ACFC0_header, fn_803ACFC0_write_block, fn_803ADF90_read, CARD_QUEUE_CMD, CARD_WRITE_BLOCK.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: hsd_3AA7.c helper and macro additions

naming_conventions.text: New axdriver data symbols keep address-style names AXDriver_803B95F8 and AXDriver_803B9618 rather than invented semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: axdriver.c const RevHiDims AXDriver_803B95F8 and const s32 AXDriver_803B9618[]

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit PAD_STACK only with validation evidence; this PR introduces PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(68), and PAD_STACK(64) in hsd_3AA7.c and the PR body reports clean build/diff/change sweeps.
evidence_refs: pr_body: ninja, ninja dol_diff, ninja changes_all, regressions=0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust local declaration order and local lifetimes in complex CARD functions as a narrow matching tactic; hsd_3AA7.c reorders locals such as result/retries/buf/sector_size/data/offset and introduces saved/file_size/callback_seq ordering changes.
Try source-level control-flow reshaping before accepting generated-looking loops: examples include do-while retry/slot loops, bounded pass loops, early break insertion, and 1-based block iteration.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: hsd_3AA7.c for-pass loop and block iteration changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline helper boundaries can be a matching tactic for repeated pointer arithmetic and CARD I/O retry loops, as shown by the hsd_3AA7.c helper additions.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: fn_803ACFC0_write_block wraps CARDWrite retry loop

accepted_candidate_records.title: hsd_3AA7.c used local inline helpers to isolate repeated CARD/block operations
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2732, hsd_3AA7.c introduced local inline helpers around block-count math, payload/header pointer computation, CARDWrite retry behavior, and CARDRead calls while improving several hsd_3AA7 functions per the bot report. Treat this as a PR-local matching pattern candidate, not a global rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK retained with PR-level validation in hsd_3AA7.c
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2732 added PAD_STACK in multiple hsd_3AA7.c functions and the PR body reports clean build, dol_diff, changes_all, and regressions=0. This is useful as evidence that stack-shape tactics appeared in the PR, but curator should avoid promoting PAD_STACK as style beyond the existing evidence-required standard.

accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2732 used operation-scoped local helper names where behavior was localized and retained address-style names for new axdriver const data symbols, consistent with conservative naming when semantics are not fully proven.
